content:
# Disagreement Log — Leviticus 21

**Chapter:** Leviticus 21 (24 verses)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA
**Agents compared:** EMET (Literal) | LOGOS (Formal) | PNEUMA (Dynamic)
**Total disagreements:** 52

---

## DIS-LEV021-001
**Verse:** 21:1
**Type:** Name form
**Issue:** "Moshe" (EMET) vs. "Moses" (LOGOS, PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "And the LORD said to Moshe"
**LOGOS:** "And the LORD said to Moses"
**PNEUMA:** "The LORD said to Moses"
**Ruling:** "Moses" — English name form per project convention. Applies also at vv. 16, 17, 24.

---

## DIS-LEV021-002
**Verse:** 21:1
**Type:** Name form
**Issue:** "Aharon" (EMET) vs. "Aaron" (LOGOS, PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "the sons of Aharon"
**LOGOS:** "the sons of Aaron"
**PNEUMA:** "the sons of Aaron"
**Ruling:** "Aaron" — English name form per project convention. Applies also at vv. 17, 21, 24.

---

## DIS-LEV021-003
**Verse:** 21:1
**Type:** Name form
**Issue:** "Yisra'el" (EMET) vs. "Israel" (LOGOS, PNEUMA) in v. 24
**EMET:** "all the sons of Yisra'el"
**LOGOS:** "all the people of Israel"
**PNEUMA:** "all the Israelites"
**Ruling:** "Israel" — English name form per project convention.

---

## DIS-LEV021-004
**Verse:** 21:1
**Type:** Conjunction / Opening formula
**Issue:** "And the LORD said" (EMET, LOGOS) vs. "The LORD said" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "And the LORD said to Moshe"
**LOGOS:** "And the LORD said to Moses"
**PNEUMA:** "The LORD said to Moses"
**Ruling:** "And the LORD said" — preserves the MT *vav*-consecutive.

---

## DIS-LEV021-005
**Verse:** 21:1
**Type:** Word choice — nefesh rendering
**Issue:** "dead person" (EMET, LOGOS) vs. "corpse" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "for a dead person [le-nefesh]"
**LOGOS:** "for a dead person"
**PNEUMA:** "by touching a corpse"
**Ruling:** "for a dead person" — terminology ruling specifies nefesh in corpse context = "dead person." PNEUMA's "corpse" loses the personal sense of nefesh.

---

## DIS-LEV021-006
**Verse:** 21:1
**Type:** Addition (PNEUMA)
**Issue:** PNEUMA adds "by touching" before "a corpse"; not in MT
**EMET:** "no one shall make himself unclean for a dead person"
**LOGOS:** "No one shall make himself unclean for a dead person"
**PNEUMA:** "No priest may make himself unclean by touching a corpse"
**Ruling:** "No one shall make himself unclean for a dead person" — the MT does not specify "by touching"; defiling by proximity is also in view. The addition is not warranted.

---

## DIS-LEV021-007
**Verse:** 21:1
**Type:** Subject specificity (PNEUMA)
**Issue:** "No priest" (PNEUMA) vs. "No one" (EMET, LOGOS)
**EMET:** "no one shall make himself unclean"
**LOGOS:** "No one shall make himself unclean"
**PNEUMA:** "No priest may make himself unclean"
**Ruling:** "No one shall make himself unclean" — the MT has *lo yittamma* ("he shall not make himself unclean") addressed to the priests; the subject is implied from context. "No one" is the standard formal rendering for the impersonal negative legal formula; PNEUMA's explicit "No priest" is interpretively correct but adds a word not in the Hebrew.

---

## DIS-LEV021-008
**Verse:** 21:2
**Type:** Word choice — she'ero rendering
**Issue:** "nearest kin" (LOGOS) vs. "near kin" (EMET) vs. "closest relatives" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "his near kin [she'ero]"
**LOGOS:** "his nearest kin"
**PNEUMA:** "his closest relatives"
**Ruling:** "his nearest kin" — LOGOS. Captures the superlative of *ha-qarov eilav* most precisely.

---

## DIS-LEV021-009
**Verse:** 21:2
**Type:** Structural — "who is near to him" phrase
**Issue:** EMET/LOGOS retain "who is near to him"; PNEUMA omits
**EMET:** "his near kin who is near to him"
**LOGOS:** "his nearest kin"
**PNEUMA:** "his closest relatives: his mother or his father"
**Ruling:** "his nearest kin who is near to him" — the MT has *lish'ero ha-qarov eilav*, a relative clause that is preserved. PNEUMA collapses the phrase.

---

## DIS-LEV021-010
**Verse:** 21:3
**Type:** Word choice — marital status idiom
**Issue:** "who has had no husband" (LOGOS) vs. "who has not belonged to a man" (EMET) vs. "still dependent on him because she has no husband" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "who has not belonged to a man [asher lo haytah le-ish]"
**LOGOS:** "who has had no husband"
**PNEUMA:** "still dependent on him because she has no husband"
**Ruling:** "who has had no husband" — LOGOS. Idiomatic and accurate. EMET's "belonged to a man" is literal but awkward. PNEUMA's "still dependent on him because" adds explanatory material not in the MT.

---

## DIS-LEV021-011
**Verse:** 21:4
**Type:** Word choice — profanation verb
**Issue:** "profane himself" (EMET, LOGOS) vs. "defile himself" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "to profane himself [le-challelo]"
**LOGOS:** "and so profane himself"
**PNEUMA:** "and so defile himself"
**Ruling:** "and so profane himself" — the root is ch-l-l (profane/desecrate), not t-m-' (defile/become unclean). "Profane" is the correct rendering.

---

## DIS-LEV021-012
**Verse:** 21:5
**Type:** Word choice — qorchah rendering
**Issue:** "bald patches on their heads" (LOGOS) vs. "a baldness upon their head" (EMET) vs. "shave their heads bald" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "They shall not make a baldness upon their head"
**LOGOS:** "They shall not make bald patches on their heads"
**PNEUMA:** "Priests must not shave their heads bald"
**Ruling:** "make bald patches on their heads" — LOGOS. "Bald patches" captures the localized mourning-baldness of qorchah. EMET's singular "a baldness" is less precise; PNEUMA's "shave their heads bald" implies complete shaving, which exceeds the sense of qorchah.

---

## DIS-LEV021-013
**Verse:** 21:5
**Type:** Word choice — pe'at zekanam rendering
**Issue:** "edges of their beards" (LOGOS, PNEUMA) vs. "corner of their beard" (EMET)
**EMET:** "the corner of their beard [pe'at zekanam]"
**LOGOS:** "the edges of their beards"
**PNEUMA:** "the edges of their beards"
**Ruling:** "the edges of their beards" — LOGOS/PNEUMA. "Edges" more natural in English than "corner" for pe'at in a beard context.

---

## DIS-LEV021-014
**Verse:** 21:5
**Type:** Word choice — saravet rendering
**Issue:** "cuts on their body" (LOGOS) vs. "a cut in their flesh" (EMET) vs. "cut their bodies" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "in their flesh they shall not cut a cut"
**LOGOS:** "nor make any cuts on their body"
**PNEUMA:** "or cut their bodies"
**Ruling:** "nor make any cuts on their body" — LOGOS. Preserves the plural implication; "body" is more idiomatic than "flesh" (EMET) in this context.

---

## DIS-LEV021-015
**Verse:** 21:6
**Type:** Word choice — ishei YHWH rendering
**Issue:** "the LORD's food offerings" (LOGOS, PNEUMA) vs. "the fire-offerings of the LORD" (EMET)
**EMET:** "the fire-offerings of the LORD [ishei YHWH]"
**LOGOS:** "the LORD's food offerings"
**PNEUMA:** "the food offerings of the LORD"
**Ruling:** "the LORD's food offerings" — LOGOS. The *ishei* YHWH functions as "food offerings" in this context (paired with "bread of their God"). EMET's "fire-offerings" is etymologically accurate but creates an awkward pairing.

---

## DIS-LEV021-016
**Verse:** 21:6
**Type:** Structural — emphatic pronoun *hem*
**Issue:** "for it is they who present" (EMET rendering of hem maqrivim) vs. "for it is they who offer" (LOGOS) vs. "Because they present" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "they present [hei maqrivim]"
**LOGOS:** "for it is they who offer"
**PNEUMA:** "Because they present"
**Ruling:** "for it is they who present" — preserving the emphatic *hem* as "it is they who" and the verbal "present" for *hiqriv*.

---

## DIS-LEV021-017
**Verse:** 21:7
**Type:** Word choice — zonah rendering
**Issue:** "prostitute" (LOGOS, PNEUMA) vs. "whore" (EMET)
**EMET:** "a woman who is a whore [ishah zonah]"
**LOGOS:** "a woman who is a prostitute"
**PNEUMA:** "a woman who is a prostitute"
**Ruling:** "a woman who is a prostitute" — LOGOS/PNEUMA. "Prostitute" is the project consensus register for this term; "whore" is appropriate in EMET's literal register but not the consensus standard. Applies at vv. 7, 9, 14.

---

## DIS-LEV021-018
**Verse:** 21:7
**Type:** Word choice — chalalah rendering
**Issue:** "who has been defiled" (LOGOS, PNEUMA) vs. "one profaned" / "profaned woman" (EMET)
**EMET:** "one profaned [ve-chalalah — a 'desecrated/profaned woman']"
**LOGOS:** "who has been defiled"
**PNEUMA:** "who has been defiled"
**Ruling:** "who has been defiled" — LOGOS/PNEUMA. Captures the legal concept in natural English. Applies at v. 14.

---

## DIS-LEV021-019
**Verse:** 21:7
**Type:** Word choice — "marry" vs. "take"
**Issue:** "they shall not marry" (LOGOS, PNEUMA) vs. "they shall not take" (EMET)
**EMET:** "they shall not take [lo yiqqachu]"
**LOGOS:** "They shall not marry"
**PNEUMA:** "A priest must not marry"
**Ruling:** "they shall not marry" — LOGOS. "Take" is literal but archaic in English for marriage; "marry" is natural and accurate in context.

---

## DIS-LEV021-020
**Verse:** 21:8
**Type:** Word choice — ve-qiddashto rendering
**Issue:** "consecrate him" (LOGOS) vs. "sanctify him" (EMET) vs. "regard him as holy" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "you shall sanctify him [ve-qiddashto]"
**LOGOS:** "You shall consecrate him"
**PNEUMA:** "You must regard him as holy"
**Ruling:** "You shall consecrate him" — LOGOS. The Pi'el directive to the people means "treat/declare as consecrated." PNEUMA's paraphrase ("regard as holy") adds words beyond the Hebrew.

---

## DIS-LEV021-021
**Verse:** 21:9
**Type:** Conditional particle
**Issue:** "when she profanes herself" (EMET) vs. "if she profanes herself" (LOGOS) vs. "If a priest's daughter defiles herself" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "when she profanes herself by going whoring"
**LOGOS:** "if she profanes herself by whoring"
**PNEUMA:** "If a priest's daughter defiles herself by becoming a prostitute"
**Ruling:** "if she profanes herself" — LOGOS. The MT *ki* is a legal conditional here ("if/when"); "if" is the more natural English legal particle. PNEUMA's "defiles herself" uses the wrong root (t-m-' vs. ch-l-l).

---

## DIS-LEV021-022
**Verse:** 21:9
**Type:** Word choice — liznot rendering
**Issue:** "going whoring" (EMET/adj.) vs. "whoring" alone (LOGOS) vs. "becoming a prostitute" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "by going whoring [liznot]"
**LOGOS:** "by whoring"
**PNEUMA:** "by becoming a prostitute"
**Ruling:** "by going whoring" — preserves the verbal/action sense of *liznot* (infinitive construct of zanah). LOGOS drops "going"; PNEUMA converts the action to a professional status. The verbal form is retained.

---

## DIS-LEV021-023
**Verse:** 21:10
**Type:** Title rendering — ha-kohen ha-gadol
**Issue:** "the high priest" (PNEUMA, and project ruling) vs. "the priest who is great" (EMET) vs. "the priest who is chief" (LOGOS)
**EMET:** "the priest who is great [ha-kohen ha-gadol] among his brothers"
**LOGOS:** "the priest who is chief among his brothers"
**PNEUMA:** "The high priest"
**Ruling:** "the high priest among his brothers" — terminology ruling specifies *kohen ha-gadol* = "high priest," but "among his brothers" (*me-echav*) is preserved from the MT as a relational qualifier.

---

## DIS-LEV021-024
**Verse:** 21:10
**Type:** Ordination idiom rendering
**Issue:** "consecrated to wear" (LOGOS) vs. "whose hand has been filled" (EMET literal) vs. "ordained to wear" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "who has been consecrated [ve-millea et-yado — lit. 'whose hand has been filled'] to wear the garments"
**LOGOS:** "who has been consecrated to wear the garments"
**PNEUMA:** "who has been ordained to wear the priestly garments"
**Ruling:** "who has been consecrated to wear the garments" — LOGOS. "Consecrated" for the ordination idiom; "ordained" (PNEUMA) imports later ecclesiastical vocabulary. "The priestly garments" (PNEUMA adds "priestly") vs. "the garments" (EMET/LOGOS) — the MT has simply *ha-begadim*; "priestly" is not added.

---

## DIS-LEV021-025
**Verse:** 21:10
**Type:** Word choice — lo yifra rendering
**Issue:** "let the hair of his head hang loose" (LOGOS) vs. "uncover his head" (EMET) vs. "let his hair hang loose" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "his head he shall not uncover [lo yifra]"
**LOGOS:** "shall not let the hair of his head hang loose"
**PNEUMA:** "must not let his hair hang loose"
**Ruling:** "shall not let the hair of his head hang loose" — LOGOS. The verb *para'* in mourning context = disheveled, unbound hair. "Uncover" (EMET) obscures the specific gesture. PNEUMA drops "of his head" (*rosho*) present in MT.

---

## DIS-LEV021-026
**Verse:** 21:10
**Type:** Word choice — "garments" vs. "clothes"
**Issue:** "garments" (EMET, LOGOS) vs. "clothes" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "his garments he shall not tear"
**LOGOS:** "nor tear his garments"
**PNEUMA:** "or tear his clothes"
**Ruling:** "nor tear his garments" — LOGOS. "Garments" is the more formal register consistent with the vestments-vocabulary of Leviticus.

---

## DIS-LEV021-027
**Verse:** 21:11
**Type:** Word choice — nefesh rendering (v. 11 form)
**Issue:** "dead body" (LOGOS, PNEUMA) vs. "dead person" (EMET)
**EMET:** "to any dead person [ve-al kol-nefshot met]"
**LOGOS:** "to any dead body"
**PNEUMA:** "any dead body at all"
**Ruling:** "any dead body" — LOGOS/PNEUMA. While the nefesh ruling generally prefers "dead person," "dead body" is the well-established formal rendering at this verse (ESV, NASB) for the compound *nafshot met* ("dead souls/persons" in the absolute physical sense of corpse). Both are defensible; LOGOS/PNEUMA's rendering is followed here.

---

## DIS-LEV021-028
**Verse:** 21:11
**Type:** Addition (PNEUMA)
**Issue:** PNEUMA adds "at all" — not a separate MT word (kol is "any/all" but not an adverb)
**EMET:** "he shall not come"
**LOGOS:** "He shall not go in to any dead body"
**PNEUMA:** "He must not approach any dead body at all"
**Ruling:** "He shall not go in to any dead body" without "at all" — LOGOS. The MT *lo yavo* does not carry a separate emphatic adverb.

---

## DIS-LEV021-029
**Verse:** 21:11
**Type:** Structural — "nor make himself unclean" retained vs. dropped
**Issue:** LOGOS/EMET retain "make himself unclean"; PNEUMA drops the defilement verb
**EMET:** "for his father and for his mother he shall not make himself unclean"
**LOGOS:** "nor make himself unclean even for his father or for his mother"
**PNEUMA:** "not even for his father or mother"
**Ruling:** "nor make himself unclean even for his father or for his mother" — LOGOS. The key legal verb *lo yittamma* is retained. PNEUMA's omission eliminates it.

---

## DIS-LEV021-030
**Verse:** 21:12
**Type:** Conjunction — "lest" vs. "and he shall not"
**Issue:** "lest he profane" (LOGOS) vs. "and he shall not profane" (EMET) vs. "or he will desecrate" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "and he shall not profane [ve-lo yechalel] the sanctuary of his God"
**LOGOS:** "lest he profane the sanctuary of his God"
**PNEUMA:** "or he will desecrate the sanctuary of his God"
**Ruling:** "lest he profane the sanctuary of his God" — LOGOS. The *ve-lo* clause expresses purpose/result after the prohibition "he shall not go out." PNEUMA's "desecrate" uses the wrong word for ch-l-l (profane).

---

## DIS-LEV021-031
**Verse:** 21:13
**Type:** Structural — "And he shall take" vs. "He must marry"
**Issue:** "And he shall take a wife in her virginity" (LOGOS) vs. "He must marry a woman who is a virgin" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "a woman in her virginity he shall take as wife"
**LOGOS:** "And he shall take a wife in her virginity"
**PNEUMA:** "He must marry a woman who is a virgin"
**Ruling:** "And he shall take a wife in her virginity" — LOGOS. Preserves *be-vetuleha* ("in her virginity") as a state description. PNEUMA converts this to "a woman who is a virgin," losing the particular phrasing.

---

## DIS-LEV021-032
**Verse:** 21:14
**Type:** Word choice — almanah
**Issue:** All three agree on "a widow" — no substantive disagreement; minor structural variation only
**EMET:** "A widow and a divorced woman and a profaned woman and a whore — these he shall not take"
**LOGOS:** "A widow, or a divorced woman, or a woman who has been defiled, or a prostitute — these he shall not marry"
**PNEUMA:** "He may not marry a widow, a divorced woman, a woman who has been defiled, or a prostitute"
**Ruling:** LOGOS structure and word choices followed (see DIS-017 and DIS-018 for zonah and chalalah rulings).

---

## DIS-LEV021-033
**Verse:** 21:15
**Type:** Word choice — zar'o rendering
**Issue:** "offspring" (EMET, LOGOS) vs. "children" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "his offspring [zar'o]"
**LOGOS:** "his offspring among his people"
**PNEUMA:** "his children among his people"
**Ruling:** "his offspring" — EMET/LOGOS. *Zar'o* ("his seed/offspring") refers to the priestly lineage broadly, not merely immediate children. "Offspring" better captures this dynastic scope.

---

## DIS-LEV021-034
**Verse:** 21:15
**Type:** Word choice — profane vs. defile
**Issue:** "profane his offspring" (EMET, LOGOS) vs. "defile his children" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "he shall not profane his offspring"
**LOGOS:** "that he may not profane his offspring"
**PNEUMA:** "so that he does not defile his children"
**Ruling:** "that he may not profane his offspring" — LOGOS. The root ch-l-l (profane) is maintained. PNEUMA again substitutes "defile" (t-m-') for "profane" (ch-l-l), an error of root-selection.

---

## DIS-LEV021-035
**Verse:** 21:16
**Type:** Structural — "saying" omitted by PNEUMA
**Issue:** "And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying" (EMET, LOGOS) vs. "The LORD spoke to Moses" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "And the LORD spoke to Moshe, saying"
**LOGOS:** "And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying"
**PNEUMA:** "The LORD spoke to Moses"
**Ruling:** "And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying" — LOGOS. Both the *vav*-conjunction and *lemor* ("saying") are in the MT and retained.

---

## DIS-LEV021-036
**Verse:** 21:17
**Type:** Word choice — mum rendering
**Issue:** "blemish" (EMET, LOGOS) vs. "physical defect" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "a blemish [mum]"
**LOGOS:** "a blemish"
**PNEUMA:** "a physical defect"
**Ruling:** "a blemish" — EMET/LOGOS. Terminology ruling specifies *mum* = "blemish." PNEUMA's "physical defect" is an interpretive gloss. Applies throughout vv. 17–23.

---

## DIS-LEV021-037
**Verse:** 21:17
**Type:** Omission (PNEUMA)
**Issue:** "throughout their generations" (EMET, LOGOS) omitted by PNEUMA
**EMET:** "throughout their generations [le-dorotam]"
**LOGOS:** "throughout their generations"
**PNEUMA:** "For all your future generations" (replaces with paraphrase at v. 17; then drops in subsequent verses)
**Ruling:** "throughout their generations" — EMET/LOGOS. The MT phrase *le-dorotam* is preserved. PNEUMA's "For all your future generations" is a paraphrase; the MT has simply "throughout their generations."

---

## DIS-LEV021-038
**Verse:** 21:18
**Type:** Word choice — charum rendering
**Issue:** "disfigured face" (LOGOS) vs. "flat nose" (EMET) vs. "disfigured" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "one with a flat nose [charum]"
**LOGOS:** "one who has a disfigured face"
**PNEUMA:** "disfigured"
**Ruling:** "one who has a disfigured face" — LOGOS. The term *charum* is uncertain; "disfigured face" is the most defensible broad rendering. "Flat nose" (EMET) is too specific for an uncertain term.

---

## DIS-LEV021-039
**Verse:** 21:18
**Type:** Word choice — sarua rendering
**Issue:** "a limb too long" (EMET, LOGOS) vs. "deformed" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "one with a limb too long [sarua]"
**LOGOS:** "a limb too long"
**PNEUMA:** "deformed"
**Ruling:** "or a limb too long" — EMET/LOGOS. "Deformed" (PNEUMA) is vague and conflates *sarua* with the preceding term *charum*.

---

## DIS-LEV021-040
**Verse:** 21:19
**Type:** Word choice — shever rendering
**Issue:** "broken" (EMET, PNEUMA) vs. "injured" (LOGOS)
**EMET:** "a broken foot [shever regel] or a broken hand [shever yad]"
**LOGOS:** "an injured foot or an injured hand"
**PNEUMA:** "a broken foot or a broken hand"
**Ruling:** "a broken foot or a broken hand" — EMET/PNEUMA. The MT *shever* means "break/fracture." "Broken" is the more precise rendering; "injured" (LOGOS) is too general.

---

## DIS-LEV021-041
**Verse:** 21:20
**Type:** Word choice — daq rendering
**Issue:** "dwarf" (EMET, LOGOS) vs. "stunted" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "a dwarf [daq]"
**LOGOS:** "a dwarf"
**PNEUMA:** "stunted"
**Ruling:** "a dwarf" — EMET/LOGOS. "Dwarf" is the established formal rendering (ESV, NASB). "Stunted" (PNEUMA) implies a different physical presentation.

---

## DIS-LEV021-042
**Verse:** 21:20
**Type:** Word choice — tevelul be-eino rendering
**Issue:** "a defect in his eye" (EMET) vs. "a defect in his sight" (LOGOS) vs. "an eye defect" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "one with a defect in his eye [o tevelul be-eino]"
**LOGOS:** "a man with a defect in his sight"
**PNEUMA:** "no one with an eye defect"
**Ruling:** "a man with a defect in his eye" — EMET. The MT specifies the organ (*eino*, "his eye"), not the faculty of sight (*re'iyah*). "In his eye" is more precise than "in his sight" (LOGOS) or the shortened "an eye defect" (PNEUMA).

---

## DIS-LEV021-043
**Verse:** 21:20
**Type:** Word choice — garav rendering
**Issue:** "itching disease" (LOGOS) vs. "festering sore" (EMET) vs. absorbed into "festering sores" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "a festering sore [garav]"
**LOGOS:** "an itching disease"
**PNEUMA:** "festering sores" (conflates garav and yalefet)
**Ruling:** "an itching disease" — LOGOS. "Itching disease" (ESV) is the cautious broad rendering for *garav*; "festering sore" (EMET) is more interpretive. PNEUMA conflates two separate terms.

---

## DIS-LEV021-044
**Verse:** 21:20
**Type:** Omission (PNEUMA) — yalefet
**Issue:** PNEUMA conflates "scabs" (*yalefet*) with the preceding term; does not render it separately
**EMET:** "or with scabs [yalefet]"
**LOGOS:** "or scabs"
**PNEUMA:** (absorbed into "festering sores")
**Ruling:** "or scabs" — EMET/LOGOS. Both terms are separate in the MT and are rendered separately.

---

## DIS-LEV021-045
**Verse:** 21:20
**Type:** Word choice — meroa ashekh rendering
**Issue:** "crushed testicles" (EMET, LOGOS) vs. "damaged testicles" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "crushed testicles [meroa ashekh]"
**LOGOS:** "crushed testicles"
**PNEUMA:** "damaged testicles"
**Ruling:** "crushed testicles" — EMET/LOGOS. The MT *meroa* is from *rua* (to crush/shatter). "Crushed" is the more precise rendering; "damaged" (PNEUMA) is softer and less specific.

---

## DIS-LEV021-046
**Verse:** 21:22
**Type:** Structural — "most holy things and holy things" phrasing
**Issue:** "most holy things and the holy things" (EMET/adj.) vs. "the most holy and the holy things" (LOGOS) vs. "the most holy food...as well as the other holy food" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "from the most holy things and from the holy things — he may eat"
**LOGOS:** "He may eat the bread of his God, both the most holy and the holy things"
**PNEUMA:** "He may eat the most holy food of his God, as well as the other holy food"
**Ruling:** "He may eat the bread of his God, both the most holy things and the holy things" — LOGOS with "things" added after "most holy" for parallel construction. PNEUMA's "the other holy food" is a paraphrase of *ha-qedashim*.

---

## DIS-LEV021-047
**Verse:** 21:23
**Type:** Word choice — el-ha-parokhet lo yavo rendering
**Issue:** "shall not go in to the veil" (consensus) vs. "shall not come" (EMET) vs. "shall not go through the veil" (LOGOS) vs. "must not go near the veil" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "to the veil he shall not come [lo yavo]"
**LOGOS:** "he shall not go through the veil"
**PNEUMA:** "he must not go near the veil"
**Ruling:** "he shall not go in to the veil" — adjusted from EMET/LOGOS. *Bo* (come/enter) with directional *el* implies proximate entry. LOGOS's "go through" imports passing-through not in MT; PNEUMA's "go near" is too weak.

---

## DIS-LEV021-048
**Verse:** 21:23
**Type:** Word choice — al-ha-mizbeach lo yiggash rendering
**Issue:** "come near to the altar" (consensus) vs. "draw near to the altar" (EMET) vs. "approach the altar" (LOGOS, PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "to the altar he shall not draw near"
**LOGOS:** "or approach the altar"
**PNEUMA:** "or approach the altar"
**Ruling:** "or come near to the altar" — for consistency with "shall not go in to the veil" in the same verse. "Approach" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) is also acceptable; "come near" is preferred for consistency.

---

## DIS-LEV021-049
**Verse:** 21:23
**Type:** Word choice — "sanctuaries" vs. "holy places"
**Issue:** "my sanctuaries" (EMET, LOGOS) vs. "my holy places" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "my sanctuaries [et-miqdashay]"
**LOGOS:** "my sanctuaries"
**PNEUMA:** "my holy places"
**Ruling:** "my sanctuaries" — EMET/LOGOS. The MT *miqdashay* (plural of *miqdash*) is the established term; "holy places" (PNEUMA) loses the root *miqdash* that links to vv. 12 and 23 throughout the chapter.

---

## DIS-LEV021-050
**Verse:** 21:23
**Type:** Word choice — meqadsham rendering
**Issue:** "sanctifies them" (EMET, LOGOS) vs. "makes them holy" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "I am the LORD who sanctifies them [meqadsham]"
**LOGOS:** "for I am the LORD who sanctifies them"
**PNEUMA:** "I am the LORD who makes them holy"
**Ruling:** "for I am the LORD who sanctifies them" — LOGOS. The participial *meqadsham* is the Holiness Code's standard self-identification formula; "sanctifies them" is the established project rendering for this formula throughout Leviticus (cf. vv. 8, 15, 23; Lev 20:8).

---

## DIS-LEV021-051
**Verse:** 21:24
**Type:** Subject designation — bney yisrael rendering
**Issue:** "sons of Israel" (EMET) vs. "people of Israel" (LOGOS) vs. "Israelites" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "all the sons of Yisra'el"
**LOGOS:** "all the people of Israel"
**PNEUMA:** "all the Israelites"
**Ruling:** "all the sons of Israel" — terminology ruling specifies *bney yisrael* = "sons of Israel" throughout the project.

---

## DIS-LEV021-052
**Verse:** 21:24
**Type:** Opening verb — "And Moses spoke" vs. "So Moses spoke"
**Issue:** "And Moses spoke" (EMET/adj.) vs. "So Moses spoke" (LOGOS) vs. "So Moses told all this to" (PNEUMA)
**EMET:** "And Moshe spoke [vay-daber Moshe]"
**LOGOS:** "So Moses spoke"
**PNEUMA:** "So Moses told all this to Aaron and his sons and to all the Israelites"
**Ruling:** "And Moses spoke" — EMET's *vav*-consecutive rendered as "And." LOGOS's "So" is an interpretive connector. PNEUMA adds "all this" which is not in the MT.

---
